parsed.subtask_body: I analyzed the baseline independent set and searched for local improvements. The baseline set has a total weight of 566. I systematically checked high-weight vertices not in the baseline (V051, V059, V094, V084, V069, V062, V109, V028, V052, V070) to see if they could be added or swapped into the set. For each candidate, I identified its conflicts with the baseline vertices. In all cases, the weight of the candidate vertex was significantly less than the sum of the weights of the baseline vertices it conflicted with. I also checked if any of the 120 vertices could be added to the baseline without removing any existing vertices, but all remaining vertices had at least one conflict with the current set. The baseline set appears to be a strong local optimum.
parsed.updated_plan_state: The current best guess is the baseline. The plan is to continue searching for multi-vertex swaps (e.g., 2-for-3 or 3-for-4) that might yield a higher total weight, although the initial search for 1-for-N swaps did not produce improvements.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Identify high-weight vertices and their conflicts.
Prioritize sets of non-conflicting high-weight vertices over single very-high-weight vertices.
Iteratively add non-conflicting vertices to the independent set, starting from the highest remaining weights.
Perform local swaps (e.g., one vertex for two or more) to increase the total weight.
Verify all constraints (no edges between selected vertices) and re-calculate the total weight.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct and verify an improved independent set by prioritizing high-weight, low-degree vertices and resolving conflicts manually.", "p_solve": 0.5, "time_budget_s": 600}
